examples.title: E-Waste Generation Calculator
examples.description: Calculate total e-waste generation based on population and per capita generation rate
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT population, perCapitaRate
 totalEWaste = population * perCapitaRate
 
 // Calculate by category
 largeAppliances = totalEWaste * 0.30
 smallAppliances = totalEWaste * 0.25
 itEquipment = totalEWaste * 0.20
 consumerElectronics = totalEWaste * 0.15
 others = totalEWaste * 0.10
 
 OUTPUT "Total E-Waste: " + totalEWaste + " tonnes"
 OUTPUT "IT Equipment: " + itEquipment + " tonnes"
 
 // Calculate recoverable materials
 preciousMetals = itEquipment * 0.07 * 0.003
 copper = totalEWaste * 0.10 * 0.60
 
 OUTPUT "Recoverable Gold: " + preciousMetals + " kg"
 OUTPUT "Recoverable Copper: " + copper + " kg"
END
examples.explanation: Calculates total e-waste generation from population data and breaks down by category, also estimating recoverable precious metals and copper based on average composition percentages

examples.title: Material Composition Analyzer
examples.description: Analyze e-waste sample to determine material composition and hazardous content
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT eWasteWeight
 
 // Composition percentages
 ferrous = eWasteWeight * 0.22
 plastics = eWasteWeight * 0.28
 nonferrous = eWasteWeight * 0.10
 glass = eWasteWeight * 0.18
 circuitBoards = eWasteWeight * 0.07
 hazardous = eWasteWeight * 0.05
 
 // Hazardous substances breakdown
 lead = hazardous * 0.35
 mercury = hazardous * 0.08
 cadmium = hazardous * 0.10
 brominated = hazardous * 0.25
 otherHazardous = hazardous * 0.22
 
 // Check if lead exceeds safety threshold
 IF lead > (eWasteWeight * 0.01) THEN
 OUTPUT "WARNING: High lead content - Special handling required"
 ENDIF
 
 // Calculate recycling potential
 recyclableWeight = ferrous + nonferrous + glass + circuitBoards
 recyclingPotential = (recyclableWeight / eWasteWeight) * 100
 
 OUTPUT "Recycling Potential: " + recyclingPotential + "%"
 OUTPUT "Hazardous Content: " + hazardous + " kg"
END
examples.explanation: Analyzes e-waste composition to identify material types and hazardous substances, helping determine appropriate recycling and disposal methods

examples.title: Urban Mining Resource Recovery Estimator
examples.description: Estimate the economic value of recoverable materials from e-waste
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT totalEWasteTonnes
 
 // Metal recovery rates (typical percentages)
 goldRecoveryRate = 0.95
 copperRecoveryRate = 0.90
 silverRecoveryRate = 0.92
 
 // Material quantities per tonne of e-waste
 goldPerTonne = 0.00025 // 250 grams
 silverPerTonne = 0.002 // 2 kg
 copperPerTonne = 20 // 20 kg
 
 // Calculate recoverable amounts
 goldRecovered = totalEWasteTonnes * goldPerTonne * goldRecoveryRate
 silverRecovered = totalEWasteTonnes * silverPerTonne * silverRecoveryRate
 copperRecovered = totalEWasteTonnes * copperPerTonne * copperRecoveryRate
 
 // Market prices (example USD/kg)
 goldPrice = 60000
 silverPrice = 800
 copperPrice = 8
 
 // Calculate total value
 goldValue = goldRecovered * goldPrice
 silverValue = silverRecovered * silverPrice
 copperValue = copperRecovered * copperPrice
 totalValue = goldValue + silverValue + copperValue
 
 OUTPUT "Gold: " + goldRecovered + " kg = $" + goldValue
 OUTPUT "Silver: " + silverRecovered + " kg = $" + silverValue
 OUTPUT "Copper: " + copperRecovered + " kg = $" + copperValue
 OUTPUT "Total Resource Value: $" + totalValue
 OUTPUT "Per Tonne Value: $" + (totalValue / totalEWasteTonnes)
END
examples.explanation: Estimates the economic value of recoverable precious and non-ferrous metals from e-waste, demonstrating the viability of urban mining operations
